name: Account inactivity fee
summary: Fees charged for not using an account — common in gift cards, prepaid cards, bank accounts.

evidence.claim: The CARD Act of 2009 prohibits gift card issuers from charging inactivity fees until the card has been unused for at least 12 months, and requires that fee terms be prominently disclosed.

body.description: Automatic monthly fee charged when an account has no activity for a specified period. Common $1-$5/month on gift cards, $5-$10 on prepaid cards, up to $25/qtr on brokerage accounts. Gift-card inactivity fees are limited by the CARD Act (no fees for first 12 months of inactivity, E1).

disclosureLegality.requirement: CARD Act: gift-card inactivity fees barred for first 12 months; must be prominently disclosed (E1).

negotiability.script: Hi, I noticed an inactivity fee on my account. I'd like to request a waiver or transfer to an account type that doesn't charge this. Alternatively, please close the account.
